*Where you need to be:*
Race is 19 Sep — taper starts ~4 weeks out (late Aug), so peak CTL should land *mid-August*. That's 10 weeks from now. At +2 CTL/week you'd peak at *~83+20 = 103* — but that assumes zero rest weeks, which isn't realistic. *Honest assessment:*
You're on track *if* the ankle holds. The run volume is the missing piece — Z2 continuous 45 min (47 TSS today) vs where you should be (60–90 min/session, 3–4x/week, 150+ run TSS/week). Swim and bike are carrying the CTL build fine, but run fitness for a 42km IM run needs more than this by August.

Two things matter most before the July France camp: physio appointment + X-ray to confirm the bony impingement, and whether you can get a surgical consult moving. If surgery clears you to run normally by mid-July, the run block has 8 weeks — tight but workable. If not, race-day run pace will be managed to survival.

*Summary:* fitness trajectory is fine; timeline is fine; ankle diagnosis is the only variable that changes the race outcome projection.

user: It's likely just hardened fluid, ice and compression plus ankle mobility should help - from a. Friend who's a physio
assistant:
That's a reasonable starting hypothesis but it doesn't fit the test results from yesterday well.

"Hardened fluid" — chronic organized effusion — is compressible and fluctuant. What you described was *bone-hard with a thin soft layer*, which is osteophyte texture, not organized fluid. Fluid also responds to the Molloy-Bendall test (pain on dorsiflexion + pressure); yours was negative.

Ice and compression won't do much for a bony spur, but they won't cause harm either. Ankle mobility work is fine and useful regardless of diagnosis.

The physio friend hasn't examined you in person and doesn't have the test findings — their guess is lower-confidence than a hands-on assessment. Stick with the lateral X-ray in dorsiflexion as the priority. That will settle it definitively.
